stages.label: Reproduce
stages.question: Do the existing reference recipes still produce the same failures?
stages.result: Yes. Both still fail liner coverage, Cu fill, and CMP.
stages.next: Measure run-to-run variation before comparing more recipes.

stages.label: Measure noise
stages.question: How many repeats are needed for a fair comparison?
stages.result: Four repeats are enough for screening; sixteen are used for final decisions.
stages.next: Search all 17 tuning knobs across broad ranges.

stages.label: Broad screen
stages.question: Can any broad combination pass all six process steps?
stages.result: No. The best recipes pass four steps; Cu fill and CMP still fail.
stages.next: Check whether downstream effects are real or caused by different etched shapes.

stages.label: Fair comparison
stages.design: 64 downstream recipes x 4 shared shapes = 256 simulations
stages.question: Do liner, fill, and CMP results change when every recipe starts from the same etched via?
stages.result: The same fill and CMP failures remain. They were not caused by random DRIE shape differences.
stages.next: Search wider liner, barrier, fill, and CMP ranges.

stages.label: Wider search
stages.design: 128 downstream recipes x 4 shared shapes = 512 simulations
stages.question: Does a wider downstream search contain a recipe that fills the via and preserves the mask?
stages.result: No. Fill and CMP still fail. The best fill result sits at the edge of the tested range.
stages.next: Expand that edge before deciding whether the setting is truly best.

stages.label: Test the edge
stages.design: 60 boundary recipes x 4 shared shapes = 240 simulations
stages.question: Will a larger isotropic fill fraction close the centerline void?
stages.result: No. The gap stays open, and 184 simulations also remove the CMP mask.
stages.next: Stop widening this setting and retest the two best mask-safe recipes.

stages.label: Retest
stages.question: Do the two best recipes stay consistent on new etched shapes?
stages.result: The lower-dish recipe stays consistent. The smaller-gap recipe sometimes loses liner coverage.
stages.next: Perturb the stable recipe to map its nearby operating region.

stages.label: Map nearby
stages.design: 81 nearby recipes x 4 shared shapes = 324 simulations
stages.question: Is there a nearby region that improves fill and CMP without breaking earlier steps?
stages.result: 48 recipes preserve the mask, but none passes all six steps.
stages.next: Retest the best nearby recipe on sixteen new shapes.

stages.label: Confirm
stages.question: Does the nearby candidate beat the previous best recipe?
stages.result: No. Both pass the first four steps and fail Cu fill and CMP in all 32 runs.
stages.next: Change the fill and CMP models before spending more compute on these same settings.
